script:
Hello, this is The Portfolio Podcast for MEME. Monday — here is your MEME recap. The portfolio closed two point five five percent up on the session. Nine primary holdings. One name stole the entire session. Let's get into it.

IREN Limited posted a nineteen point five seven percent single-session gain. That is not a rounding error. That is one of the largest single-day moves in this portfolio this year, and it happened on a Monday with no broad macro catalyst to lean on.

No macro news shaped Monday's broad session, and index-level data was limited. The Nasdaq continued its pattern of moderate upside, with technology and communication services among the leading sectors. In the absence of a major macro print or Federal Reserve commentary, individual stock catalysts dominated price action across the portfolio.

The top story belongs to Nebius Group. The company announced a seven hundred seventy-five million dollar senior secured debt facility, backed by GPU assets and contracted revenue from partners including Microsoft and Meta. This is the company's first major asset-backed financing deal, and the structure matters. Lenders agreed to collateralize against live GPU infrastructure and existing contracts — not future projections. That is a fundamentally different risk profile than unsecured growth-stage debt. The market responded with a two point seven six percent gain on the session. The headline from Yahoo Finance framed it directly: most AI debt deals spook investors. This one did the opposite. For holders, the story here is not just the capital raised — it is what the deal structure signals about contract quality and asset durability inside Nebius Group's AI cloud build-out.

Now the full holdings rundown, largest weight first.

Applied Optoelectronics was up zero point six zero percent on the session. The move was modest relative to peers in the optical networking space, but the direction was positive. The name remains leveraged to data center transceiver demand, and any acceleration in AI infrastructure spending is a direct tailwind. Watch for any commentary out of large cloud providers in the coming weeks.

Redwire Corporation was up one point seven eight percent. The company announced a major expansion of its Huntsville, Alabama campus — adding one hundred sixty-four thousand square feet of manufacturing and engineering space, focused on unmanned aircraft systems and space infrastructure. The announcement came jointly with the State of Alabama and the City of Huntsville, and was made during the Farnborough Airshow. A separate analyst piece noted some red flags alongside the long-term upside case, so the fundamental picture remains mixed. Production capacity expansion is a concrete operational development worth tracking.

Turning to AST SpaceMobile, down zero point six six percent on the session. Revenue growth expectations remain a headline item — one analyst piece cited growth projections significantly ahead of SpaceX over the near-term horizon. A separate piece was notably skeptical, pointing to major execution hurdles despite the company's one billion dollar capital raise. The divergence in analyst sentiment on this name is wide, and that spread is worth monitoring.

Nebius Group, as covered in the top story, was up two point seven six percent. The seven hundred seventy-five million dollar debt facility is the defining catalyst. Watch for any follow-on analyst revisions.

Bloom Energy was down eight point three three percent on the session. A Yahoo Finance piece framed it as a direct split trade against FuelCell Energy, which rallied six percent on the same day. The article described investors being forced to pick a side in the fuel cell space. No specific fundamental news drove the Bloom Energy decline — the move appears to reflect relative sector rotation rather than a company-specific development. That distinction matters for how holders interpret the move.

Over at TeraWulf, up three point eight five percent. No direct company news drove the session, but the broader Bitcoin mining and AI infrastructure adjacency continues to shape sentiment around this name. The move was one of the stronger performers in the portfolio outside of IREN Limited.

Lumentum Holdings was up four point four seven percent, making it the second-strongest performer among holdings with direct news catalysts. Barclays upgraded the stock to Overweight from Equal Weight on Monday, reiterating a price target of one thousand dollars. The analyst cited Lumentum's position in the AI trade after a rough stretch earlier in the year. The stock was also cited in a broader market wrap as near the top of the S&P five hundred for the session. A Barclays upgrade with a specific price target is a concrete catalyst.

IREN Limited was up nineteen point five seven percent — the headline number of the entire session. A news item tied to the company noted a lifted AI cloud revenue target alongside upgraded contracts with AI industry leaders. The magnitude of this move in a single session is significant for overall portfolio performance, and it is the primary reason the portfolio finished up two point five five percent.

Sandisk Corporation was up two point six seven percent on the session. No direct company news was available. The move aligns with broader strength in technology names on Monday and positions the stock constructively heading into any upcoming storage sector data points.

Three items to watch in the next forty-eight hours. First, any follow-on analyst commentary on Nebius Group following the debt facility announcement — the initial reaction was positive, but institutional coverage will shape the next move. Second, watch for Lumentum-related order flow data or customer commentary following the Barclays upgrade. Third, monitor any update from IREN Limited on the specifics of its lifted AI cloud revenue target and contract structure.

This is The Portfolio Podcast for MEME. We will be back same time the next business day with tomorrow's news. Watch whether Monday's momentum in IREN Limited and Lumentum Holdings carries into Tuesday's open, and whether Bloom Energy stabilizes or extends its decline.
